sourceMovie.title: Ghost World
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Ghost World for the same reason it broke through in 2001: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Terry Zwigoff keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Edge of Seventeen
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Ghost World clicked for you, The Edge of Seventeen (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Ghost World?
faq.answer: Try The Edge of Seventeen, Now and Then, Boys on the Side—each shares DNA with Ghost World while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Ghost World?
faq.answer: Ghost World (2001) is best described as Comedy, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Ghost World worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
